summary: Conjecture 5.3 asks whether large dom(T) forces a bounded-size subtournament with large clique number ω̄. Crew, Fan, Koerts, Moore, and Spirkl (arXiv:2602.09863, 2026) prove Corollary 7 of their paper, which is exactly Conjecture 5.8 of the source paper: every tournament T with ω̄(T) ≥ f(k) contains a subtournament X of size at most ℓ(k) with ω̄(X) ≥ k. Since Property 3.2 of the source paper establishes dom(T) ≤ ω̄(T) for every tournament T, Conjecture 5.3 follows immediately as a corollary: dom(T) ≥ f(k) implies ω̄(T) ≥ f(k), after which Corollary 7 applies.

since_posted.title: Characterizing Large Clique Number in Tournaments
since_posted.authors: Logan Crew, Xinyue Fan, Hidde Koerts, Benjamin Moore, Sophie Spirkl
since_posted.claim: Proves Corollary 7 (= Conjecture 5.8 of Aboulker et al.): every tournament with sufficiently large ω̄ contains a bounded-size subtournament with large ω̄; since dom(T) ≤ ω̄(T), this immediately resolves Conjecture 5.3.

search_queries: tournament "clique number" "dom" subtournament cluster Aboulker Aubian Charbit Lopes 2310.04265
notes: arXiv:2602.09863 explicitly targets Conjecture 5.8 of the source paper (its Corollary 7), not Conjecture 5.3 directly; however, Conjecture 5.3 is an immediate logical consequence via the inequality dom(T) ≤ ω̄(T) ≤ χ̄(T) established as Property 3.2 in the source paper.
